item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: As the second image could not be sourced it is assumed that the image is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and has been removed.

item.title: Image source
item.content:encoded:
Turtle: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Desert_tortoise.jpg

available under Creative Commons.

Blue sphere: no idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ive been to the area near and around the "Bolson de Mapimi" described. A lot of it is known as "the zone of silence" in Spanish, because there is a lot of radio and electromagnetic interference, causing a lot of radio and phone signals to dissappear. Its believed this has to do with the electromagnetic field produced by the unusually large amount of asteroids that hit the area. Im guessing the author was probably inspired by that to write this.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good to see that the bad number is taken up with wholesome (by SCP standards, anyway) turtles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know it's an old post but…

1) having no idea how big the site is within the foundation universe, a containment area the size of a football field seems neither reasonable nor unreasonable (in an earlier draft I had a note prohibiting JR's from racing the tortoises but it was too silly to leave in)

2) it says right in the SCP that they tried breeding non-anomalous tortoises from anomalous ones and it actually worked, so maybe the healthy generation is being released back into the wild. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I no like. Not down voting, since I no like for stupid reasons, but I no like.

The idea of the Foundation keeping nearly a tenth of the potentional breeding population, and thus genetics, of an endangered species in captivity just makes this tortoise owner unhappy. And, unless they've devoted an entire airplane hanger to these guys, they don't have enough room to properly house a captive population. Hell, my 6" Russian books it across his 4' enclosure in no time flat when he wants to, now multiply that by 12+ and yeah, you need at least half football field…for one.

So no vote, cuz I'm a pedantic tortoise parent owner.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bit late, but wouldn't it be a paleontological excavation/finding? Archaeological sites or excavations are strictly searching for man-made objects. Granted, archaeologists would initially confuse it as man-made, but…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reverse-borg turtles is a cool idea, but this doesn't really do anything with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: it's not meant to be, but a connection could still be made…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 0_o I just realized…. could this be related to the mars turtles?! Alien turtle conspiracy!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I will simply reduce the cause of my +1 to a simple meme:

"I liek turtlez".

Now talking serious, I love the fact that somekind of synthetic organism adds itself or manages to create a bionically enhanced biollogical organism.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like that they're apparently evolving to be more organic over time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This hits this nice sweet-spot of weird for me. It's highly anomalous, quirky, with enough WTF/sec to intrigue, but not so much as to become incomprehensible. I wonder about what the hell is going on, without feeling like I'm missing things.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fun. The last addendum makes the whole thing just a little creepy around the edges.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: honestly I have no idea why I censored those. de-censoring.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Only complaint: footnote 3, and the percentage in paragraph 2 of the description. Either censor the whole thing, or not at all. However, I don't see this as a reason to downvote, and in fact quite like it. Good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Bionic tortoises.

Oh yes. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: fixed, thanks :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Click on "Files" at the bottom of the page and follow instructions to upload the pic to the page, then change the image link in the article to point to the pic here instead of off-site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i have no idea how to host them on the site. last time it was either automatic or someone did it for me. Help?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but I can't see the pictures. Can someone save them and get them hosted on site, please? Author? Mod? anyone?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't care for the idea. It's boring to me. Maybe I just don't get it. I am lacking in sleep after all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Great core idea with concise, no-frills execution.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is simple and interesting. I think the core idea is novel, and has a discreet hook. I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alright. I thought it would be cute just because of the several levels of irony involved (here's basically an extraterrestrial lander program, being cleaned up by robots that resemble planetary landers, disguised as real landers) but several other readers have said the same thing and you've pushed it over the threshold from "meh" to "not even remotely necessary." Removing addenda…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted, but I will warn you that that reference to the government shutdown's gonna get dated reeeal fast, and wasn't necessary to begin with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Done, thanks

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Allow me to echo that statement. One minor nitpick: you have an Addendum 1488-02 and in the footnote you reference a Table 1488-02. That's kind of confusing, even if they're prefixed by "Addendum" and "Table". I'd recommend changing the footnote for clarity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sure why I like this, but I like this. It's not especially gripping or unusual, but it's capably written, and turtle biots just feel like something the SCP wiki could use.

Inexplicable upvote.
